confirmation.detail: inbox envelope env_0e16ff4f-a109-4832-b51a-56dbf57f75db read back as received

evidence: narada operator-surface agent instantiate admitted narada-cpy.builder into CPY Site registry successfully.
narada operator-surface identity add admitted narada-cpy.builder into User Site registry but wrote identity_id rather than identity_name.
Set-FocusedWindowIdentityBinding.ps1 searches registry.identities by $_.identity_name and would not find the new record shape.
Build-WindowLabelsFromIdentities.ps1 also reads identity_name, role_metadata, narada_site_relation, and label_projection.
payload.local_repair: Added compatibility fields for narada-cpy.builder in C:/Users/Andrey/Narada/operator-surfaces/identities.json and rebuilt window-labels.json.
proposal: Make Windows carrier scripts accept canonical identity_id records directly, or make narada operator-surface identity add emit a carrier-compatible projection for the current Windows User Site schema.
Add regression coverage for bind-focused / Set-FocusedWindowIdentityBinding against identity records emitted by narada operator-surface identity add.
Add an operator-surface doctor/preflight check that reports schema incompatibility before a forked builder reaches a failed bind step.
payload.summary: Admitting narada-cpy.builder through narada operator-surface identity add produced a modern identity_id-shaped record, while current Windows carrier scripts still require legacy identity_name, role_metadata, narada_site_relation, and label_projection fields. The identity was not bindable until compatibility fields were manually added.
payload.title: Operator-surface identity schema mismatch between Narada CLI and Windows carrier scripts
